title: Kernelについて、soul.mdもしくはphilosophy.mdを作るべきか？方法としてはobsidianにある情報を抽出して作成する。マルチエージェント・オーケストレーションで点数化して評価せよ
summary_text: risks=authority inversion where philosophy.md starts overriding AGENTS.md/CODEX.md/prompt contracts; accidental policy drift from auto-promoting noisy or transient Beliefs into stable principles; source contamination from mixed domains (ops notes, emotional logs, campaign context) without strict eligibility; non-deterministic regeneration causing frequent diff churn and hidden behavioral changes; weak provenance links making it impossible to explain why a principle changed; missing fail-closed behavior when Belief inputs are stale or incomplete; regression blindness if writing/runtime tests do not assert worldview-source precedence and conflict rules guardrails=hard precedence contract `AGENTS/CODEX/prompts > philosophy.md > Beliefs` with explicit non-authoritative marker in philosophy.md; two-stage pipeline `Belief candidates -> reviewed delta -> manual promote` with no direct overwrite path; strict promotion schema per principle (`id, statement, why, allowed, disallowed, conflict_rule, evidence_beliefs[]`) and immutable IDs; freshness and quality gates (min evidence count, recency window, contradiction check, duplicate check) before delta generation; fail-closed on missing/invalid Belief corpus (no update, emit BLOCKED); required signed-off changelog section for every promoted/retired principle; automated static tests plus live smoke asserting philosophy path usage and no Obsidian worldview fallback during drafting minimal_safe_scope=Start with a read-only `philosophy-delta` generator that produces proposed changes from curated Beliefs and only updates philosophy.md after explicit human approval with full evidence and test pass.
